query_spec.intent: Identify the longest linear citation chain among the provided papers where each paper cites the previous one in the sequence.
query_spec.selector: doc must cite another provided doc (to be the successor) or be cited by another provided doc (to be the predecessor) to form a continuous chain.

projector.ref_unit_justification: Instruction requirement 1 states: 'Please present the titles of the papers in the form of a list'.

doc_records.verdict_reason: Evidence shows DOC1 cites DOC3 ('Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding...'), making DOC3 the predecessor in the chain.
doc_records.ref_value: On the performativity of SDG classifications in large bibliometric databases
doc_records.facts: DOC1 contains a citation to 'An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation' (Su, Y. and J. Xu, 2022), which corresponds to DOC3. This establishes DOC3 -> DOC1.

outgoing_refs.quote: Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2211.10797.

doc_records.verdict_reason: Evidence shows DOC3 cites DOC2, and DOC2 does not cite any other provided doc, making it the start of the chain.
doc_records.ref_value: Contrastive Decoding: Open-ended Text Generation as Optimization
doc_records.facts: DOC2 discusses contrastive decoding and cites external works (e.g., 'Cont', 'Contrastive search is what you need'), but does not cite DOC1 or DOC3. It is cited by DOC3.

doc_records.verdict_reason: Evidence shows DOC3 cites DOC2 ('Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization') and is cited by DOC1, placing it in the middle of the chain.
doc_records.ref_value: An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation
doc_records.facts: DOC3 explicitly cites 'Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization' (Li et al., 2022), which matches DOC2. This establishes DOC2 -> DOC3.

outgoing_refs.quote: Xiang Lisa Li, Ari Holtzman, Daniel Fried, Percy Liang, Jason Eisner, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Luke Zettlemoyer, and Mike Lewis. Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization. arXiv preprint arXiv:2210.15097, 2022.

structure.summary: A linear citation chain exists where DOC2 is cited by DOC3, which is in turn cited by DOC1, forming the sequence DOC2 -> DOC3 -> DOC1.

completeness.expected_hint: Instruction asks to 'identify and list the longest citation chain' and ensure it is 'linear and continuous'.

filled_skeleton: Contrastive Decoding: Open-ended Text Generation as Optimization
An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation
On the performativity of SDG classifications in large bibliometric databases

projection_map.DOC2: Contrastive Decoding: Open-ended Text Generation as Optimization
projection_map.DOC3: An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation
